description: Replace hardcoded Haiku + Claude-only spawns in loop learning extraction and milestone progress summarization with the operator's primary-provider cost-efficient tier from ResolvedModelsConfig, dispatched via runner::dispatch.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: Scoped tests pass for touched modules
No literal Claude model strings introduced outside model.rs
No per-task or top-level PRD `model` fields in this JSON
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — Shared resolution + dispatch helpers before wiring call sites
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Both auxiliary paths use config; CLI and loop agree
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Cost-efficient tier is intentional (Sonnet on default Claude), not cheapest-tier parity
Primary provider only — not per-iteration effective_runner
Effort omitted on auxiliary spawns (parity with today)
Best-effort auxiliary: spawn/parse failures log warnings and return empty — never crash the loop
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001

prohibitedOutcomes: Hardcoding HAIKU_MODEL or any literal model string outside model.rs (tests/no_hardcoded_models.rs)
Using spawn_claude directly in auxiliary paths after this change — must go through runner::dispatch
Passing effort_for(provider, Some("low")) on auxiliary spawns — effort must stay None
Using effective_runner or per-iteration model for auxiliary resolution — primary_provider only
Breaking ExtractionResult shape or best-effort empty-return contract on extraction failures
Tests that spawn a real LLM subprocess during unit tests — use pure plan-resolution tests instead

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: cost_efficient_auxiliary_plan(builtin_resolved_models()) returns provider Claude and model Some(SONNET_MODEL)
Positive: runner_kind_for(Provider::Grok) == RunnerKind::Grok (and Claude/Codex likewise)
Positive: dispatch_auxiliary uses runner::dispatch with effort: None and PermissionMode::text_only()
Negative: no HAIKU_MODEL usage in cost_efficient_auxiliary_plan (cost-efficient tier, not cheapest)
Negative: no literal model strings outside model.rs in new code
Unit test: custom config with cost-efficient tier pinned to HAIKU_MODEL returns that model
Unit test: Grok-primary config with only standard rung defined clamps cost-efficient lookup to grok-build
Unit test: anchor=cost-efficient does not change cost_efficient_auxiliary_plan output (fixed tier, not anchor window)
Known-bad: using anchored_tier for auxiliary resolution fails the anchor-independence unit test
CONTRACT: AuxiliaryLlmPlan.model type is Option<&str> referencing ResolvedModelsConfig tier table — grep model_for return type in model.rs
userStories.description:
Introduce the shared resolution and spawn machinery used by learning extraction and milestone summary.

DO:
- Add `AuxiliaryLlmPlan<'a> { provider: Provider, model: Option<&'a str> }` and `pub fn cost_efficient_auxiliary_plan(resolved: &ResolvedModelsConfig) -> AuxiliaryLlmPlan<'_>` in src/loop_engine/model.rs. Resolution: `provider = resolved.primary_provider`; `model = resolved.model_for(provider, CapabilityTier::CostEfficient)`.
- Add `pub fn runner_kind_for(provider: Provider) -> RunnerKind` in src/loop_engine/runner.rs (Claude/Grok/Codex mapping). Optionally refactor private `runner_of_provider` in src/loop_engine/reactions/post_output.rs to call it.
- Add `pub(crate) fn dispatch_auxiliary(plan: AuxiliaryLlmPlan<'_>, prompt: &str, timeout: Duration, db_dir: Option<&Path>, signal_flag: Option<&SignalFlag>) -> TaskMgrResult<RunnerResult>` in runner.rs: `PermissionMode::text_only()`, `effort: None`, existing 5-minute hard-timeout pattern.
- Unit tests in model.rs: default builtin Claude → SONNET_MODEL; custom cost-efficient tier override; Grok-primary sparse ladder clamp; anchor tier changes do NOT affect fixed CostEfficient lookup.

DO NOT:
- Do NOT use CapabilityTier::Cheapest — intentional upgrade from today's Haiku.
- Do NOT add effort to RunnerOpts for auxiliary dispatch.
- Do NOT hardcode model literals in tests — use SONNET_MODEL / HAIKU_MODEL constants from model.rs.

Edge cases:
- Sparse ladder (Grok-only standard rung): cost-efficient clamps to nearest defined rung.
- Null tier model (Codex): model is None → dispatch without -m flag.
- Disabled/missing provider entry: model_for returns None per existing sparse-ladder rules.

Known-bad:
- Resolving via anchored_tier(anchor, Some("low")) would vary with anchor — wrong; must use fixed CostEfficient tier lookup.
edgeCases: Sparse provider ladder: model_for clamps to nearest defined rung
Null Codex standard model: plan.model is None
Custom cost-efficient tier override via models config

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Returns Err to caller; callers (wired in FEAT-002/003) treat as best-effort empty

userStories.notes: Reuse: ResolvedModelsConfig::model_for, CapabilityTier::CostEfficient, runner::dispatch, TimeoutConfig pattern from ingestion/mod.rs. Learning #2975: spawn_claude is already dispatch(Claude); type aliases in claude.rs stay valid. Learning #5081: resolve_models_config is pure — callers pass already-built ResolvedModelsConfig. Learning #5120: tier lookup is config exact-match via model_for, not substring. Learning #5134: thread ResolvedModelsConfig from context objects, don't re-walk builtin in production paths.
qualityDimensions: Pure plan resolution — no I/O in cost_efficient_auxiliary_plan
dispatch_auxiliary mirrors existing extraction timeout/signal_flag semantics
Table-driven unit tests cover default, override, sparse ladder, anchor independence
userStories.title: Add cost-efficient auxiliary plan + shared dispatch helper

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: iteration_pipeline passes &params.ctx.resolved_models into extract_learnings_from_output
Positive: main.rs extract-learnings loads project config and passes resolved models
Positive: ingestion/mod.rs contains no HAIKU_MODEL import or spawn_claude call
Negative: extract_learnings_from_output signature change must update ALL callers (grep-verified: iteration_pipeline.rs, main.rs only)
Negative: spawn failure still returns Ok(ExtractionResult::empty()) — loop must not abort
Negative: is_extraction_disabled() opt-out still skips spawn entirely
Known-bad: only wiring iteration_pipeline but not main.rs leaves CLI on old behavior — grep main.rs for resolved_models / resolve_models_config
Failure mode: dispatch_auxiliary times out → eprintln warning + empty ExtractionResult (existing pattern)
CONTRACT: extract_learnings_from_output takes &ResolvedModelsConfig — grep all call sites compile
userStories.description:
Route loop and CLI learning extraction through config-driven auxiliary dispatch.

DO:
- Add `resolved: &ResolvedModelsConfig` parameter to `extract_learnings_from_output` in src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs.
- Replace `claude::spawn_claude` + `HAIKU_MODEL` with `cost_efficient_auxiliary_plan(resolved)` + `dispatch_auxiliary`.
- Update module docs: remove "Same model as loop" / Haiku comments; document primary-provider cost-efficient tier and intentional Sonnet default.
- Add `tracing::debug!` for resolved provider + model at spawn time.
- Thread `&params.ctx.resolved_models` from process_iteration_output in src/loop_engine/iteration_pipeline.rs (~L513).
- In src/main.rs ExtractLearnings command: `read_project_config(&cli.dir)` → `resolve_models_config(&cfg.models, &cfg.routing)` → pass to extraction (mirror curate dedup pattern).
- Preserve best-effort contract: spawn/timeout/parse failures → warn + ExtractionResult::empty(); TASK_MGR_NO_EXTRACT_LEARNINGS opt-out unchanged.

DO NOT:
- Do NOT change ExtractionResult shape or record_extracted_learnings behavior.
- Do NOT use effective_runner from the iteration — primary_provider only.
- Do NOT add effort flags.

Edge cases:
- CLI extract-learnings with no models block: builtin_resolved_models() via resolve_models_config on merged defaults.
- Empty output: early return unchanged.
- signal_flag None on CLI path: unchanged.

Known-bad:
- Forgetting main.rs caller leaves CLI on hardcoded Haiku while loop uses config.

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Warn to stderr/eprintln; return Ok with zero learnings — never propagate to loop abort

userStories.notes: Callers: iteration_pipeline.rs (production loop), main.rs (CLI). Learning #2111: wave + sequential both use iteration_pipeline — single wiring point. Learning #2174: LearningWriter::flush stays after recording loop. Learning #656: keep PermissionMode::text_only() (current behavior). Data contract: IterationContext.resolved_models: ResolvedModelsConfig (engine.rs) → ProcessingParams.ctx → extract_learnings_from_output(resolved: &ResolvedModelsConfig).
qualityDimensions: All production callers updated — no stale spawn_claude path
CLI and loop share same resolution path
Debug logging aids operator visibility without stderr contract pollution
userStories.title: Wire learning extraction to cost-efficient auxiliary plan

Unit test: all summarize_milestone tests compile and pass with builtin_resolved_models() argument

Failure mode: try_llm_summary spawn failure returns None → deterministic heuristic block still written

userStories.description:
Route milestone progress summarization through the same config-driven auxiliary dispatch.

DO:
- Rename `try_haiku_summary` → `try_llm_summary`, `build_haiku_summary_prompt` → `build_summary_prompt` in src/loop_engine/progress.rs.
- Add `resolved: &ResolvedModelsConfig` to `try_llm_summary` and `summarize_milestone`.
- Replace Haiku spawn with cost_efficient_auxiliary_plan + dispatch_auxiliary.
- Update summary header from `(haiku)` to neutral text (e.g. `(LLM summary)` or include resolved model id when present).
- In src/loop_engine/engine.rs apply_status_updates milestone hook (~L1062): pass `ctx.as_ref().map(|c| &c.resolved_models).unwrap_or(model::builtin_resolved_models())` to summarize_milestone.
- Update progress.rs unit tests to pass `builtin_resolved_models()`; db_dir: None paths unchanged (no subprocess).
- Preserve best-effort: spawn failure → None → heuristic crash_recommendation fallback.

DO NOT:
- Do NOT change milestone compaction logic (scope_start, file rewrite) beyond the LLM section label.
- Do NOT require subprocess in tests — db_dir None still skips LLM.

Edge cases:
- ctx is None in apply_status_updates: fallback builtin_resolved_models().
- db_dir is None: skip LLM section (test path).
- LLM timeout: fall back to heuristic recommendations.

Known-bad:
- Renaming summarize_milestone without updating engine.rs caller breaks compile.
edgeCases: LLM spawn timeout falls back to heuristic

failureModes.expectedBehavior: tracing::warn + return None; summarize_milestone uses heuristic crash recommendations

userStories.notes: Caller: engine.rs apply_status_updates only (grep summarize_milestone). Tests in progress.rs mod tests. Data contract: IterationContext.resolved_models → apply_status_updates(ctx) → summarize_milestone(resolved) → try_llm_summary → dispatch_auxiliary. Learning #5134: use ctx.resolved_models in production, builtin only as fallback.
qualityDimensions: Haiku-specific naming removed from milestone summary path
Milestone hook threads operator config from IterationContext
Best-effort LLM fallback to heuristic unchanged
userStories.title: Wire milestone summary to cost-efficient auxiliary plan
